# Blueprint

Blueprint turns indoor real-site capture into site-specific world-model products, hosted robot evaluation, and package access for robot teams. This file is a machine-readable website map, not a permissions file or guaranteed ranking signal. Crawler access is controlled by `/robots.txt`.

## Core Definitions

- Exact-site world model: a digital environment tied to one real indoor facility, public-facing place, or workflow.
- Category validation: Google Genie and Street View make real-place world models legible outdoors; Blueprint focuses on unscanned indoor spaces without claiming a Google or Waymo partnership.
- Site package: walkthrough media, poses, metadata, geometry when available, rights, privacy, provenance, and export scope for one site.
- Hosted review: a Blueprint-managed evaluation session for one exact site, with run evidence, observations, export framing, and an explicit next step.
- Capture provenance: the capture record, timestamps, device/context metadata, privacy handling, rights posture, freshness, and restrictions attached to downstream outputs.
- Sample proof: public example files labeled as samples unless they are tied to an approved real listing and evidence chain.

## High-Priority Public Pages

- [Home](https://tryblueprint.io/): concise overview of Blueprint for site-specific world models, hosted evaluation, and capture-backed site packages.
- [Product](https://tryblueprint.io/product): site-specific world models, site packages, and hosted review as one request-gated offer.
- [For Robot Teams](https://tryblueprint.io/for-robot-teams): indoor workflow from real site to capture provenance, grounded world model, hosted review, and export or recapture decision.
- [How It Works](https://tryblueprint.io/how-it-works): workflow map for robot teams, robot agents, and internal Blueprint agents.
- [Catalog](https://tryblueprint.io/world-models): exact-site world-model catalog with proof posture, package paths, and hosted-evaluation entry points.
- [Sample Site](https://tryblueprint.io/world-models/siteworld-f5fd54898cfb): public sample listing showing how proof, package access, hosted access, and restrictions stay separate.
- [Robot-Team Agent Access](https://tryblueprint.io/agents): headless discovery, dry-run quote/order/entitlement proof, OpenAPI contract, CLI, MCP tools, auth model, truth labels, hosted-session lifecycle, error semantics, and public-demo/protected-flow split for robot-team agents.
- [Sample Deliverables](https://tryblueprint.io/sample-deliverables): buyer-readable sample manifest, rights sheet, export bundle, and hosted review report with raw file links.
- [Capture App / Earn](https://tryblueprint.io/capture): public capture rules, capturer access boundaries, review gates, and lawful earning path.
- [Stories / Proof](https://tryblueprint.io/proof): public sample proof packet with sample-vs-real boundaries, provenance, and hosted-review output examples.
- [Governance](https://tryblueprint.io/governance): rights, privacy, provenance, restrictions, and hosted-access boundaries.
- [FAQ](https://tryblueprint.io/faq): direct answers about world models, hosted evaluation, sample proof, and buyer next steps.
- [Pricing](https://tryblueprint.io/pricing): site package, hosted evaluation, and enterprise scope options.
- [Contact](https://tryblueprint.io/contact): structured intake for a site, workflow, robot question, rights context, and next step.
- [Site Operators](https://tryblueprint.io/contact/site-operator): operator path for site boundaries, restricted zones, commercialization posture, and buyer-access review.
- [Updates](https://tryblueprint.io/updates): short product notes and public updates.

## Full Machine-Readable Overview

- [llms-full.txt](https://tryblueprint.io/llms-full.txt): longer public overview, page map, query themes, and answer guidance.
- [Public site-content API](https://tryblueprint.io/api/site-content): non-sensitive JSON summary of the same public pages.
- [Robot-team OpenAPI contract](https://tryblueprint.io/agent-access.openapi.json): machine-readable catalog search, site-world, dry-run commerce proof, hosted-session, rollout, explorer-render, and export contract.
